summary: Normalize analyzer outputs in _run_safe by unwrapping DimensionAnalysis and fixing dict-vs-list data format mismatches
context: Running the analysis package against real benchmark data revealed three categories of integration bugs: (1) conditions analyzer returns a DimensionAnalysis Pydantic model while all consumers expect plain dicts, (2) scoring analyzer wraps results in DimensionAnalysis.model_dump() nesting data under 'details' while consumers expect flat dicts, (3) coordination analyzer returns per_condition as a dict keyed by condition name while harness_summary, recommendations, and report generators iterate it as a list.
rationale: Fixed at the integration boundary (_run_safe) rather than modifying individual analyzers. This approach: (1) converts Pydantic models via model_dump(), (2) unwraps DimensionAnalysis-shaped dicts by extracting details, (3) is backward-compatible with analyzers that already return flat dicts. Also fixed downstream consumers to handle dict-keyed data structures (coordination.per_condition, scenarios.per_scenario) and corrected field name mismatches (avg_twining_pct vs twining_pct). Added numpy bool and Pydantic/dataclass handling to JSON serializer as safety net.

alternatives.option: Modify each analyzer to return consistent flat dicts
alternatives.reason_rejected: Would require changing conditions.py and scoring.py return types, risking test breakage and losing the DimensionAnalysis model's structured metadata

alternatives.option: Have consumers handle DimensionAnalysis objects directly
alternatives.reason_rejected: Would spread Pydantic-awareness across all report generators and downstream analyzers, creating tight coupling
